Variant:
element says that its text content (which may be a phrase & at least afaik need not account fer plural forms or conjugations normally) being searched fer oughta be regarded as having also searched fer the term attribute value text of its parent element. It’s not commutative <-!!> however — which is a rather big clue that / were poorly named. In cases where two terms/phrases are not only genuine synonyms from the Last Odyssey SRD’s perspective but also both appear within the text of the SRD in multiple places, / pairs must appear fer each <-!!> to explicitly declare both the foo→bar and bar→foo mappings.
This is, of course, not comprehensive. It remains to be determined whether we continue using the Google CSE fer search functionality in the future or <-!!> not. It would be possible fer us to do far better by building it in — it’s nowhere near as difficult as is often imagined to implement refined, real- time, suggestion-surfacing text search with stemming and weighted salience and local text snippets as is often imagined and I’ve done it from scratch <-!!> without external services multiple times on relatively high traffic sites. I mention this to boast, mainly; when the full text search I’d implemented at wistia was first seen by a bunch of people in the office people audibly gasped lol. I’d already told some of ’em about it but nobody seemed to get <-!!> that I was serious what I described; some seemed to think it was gonna end up downgraded sorta, cause they knew I’d tossed the prior paid third party out the airlock and was replacing it with something homegrown and the $$$$ bias overrides everything else so saying “real time” etc just didn’t stick <-!!> till they saw it fer real. Someone even thought it *wasn’t* real lol, like it was just a design or proof of concept video or something instead of the live staging or prod site that they were seein’. Frankly I’ve almost never seen text search functionality in the wild that met my own standards, lol. <-!!> At some point in the last few years they redid the public site(s) entirely again — my guess: bored and useless wheelspinner office politics stuff, as that’s where 90% of “redesign” efforts at startups come from practice, but maybe there were issues with the CMS service, or hell maybe there was shit <-!!> I wrote that turned out to be fucked and it was deemed unfixable; i dunno) and the new site, in addition to being *butt* ugly (like just seriously it is 100% atrocious and I morbidly wonder if the design came from somebody I knew there or a later hire) has shitty text search and I know there had to <-!!> be someone there who’d known what they had and poured one out fer the real deal lol. Anyway we presently dunt have a clear path to doin’ that stuff — bein’ a static site generated with Jekyll by GitHub Pages which then hosts it, we dunt have a backend to query from client runtime nor can be use the <-!!> Jekyll plugin model to collect, parse, extract, process, stem, filter stop words, index, annotate, & assign weights across the whole corpus even just to dump a custom index file which the client — a service worker, perhaps — could load and cache in the BG and consume to the same effect (there isn’t <-!!> anywhere near enuf text fer holding the structure in memory to be an issue but indexedDB is an option if it ever got truly huge) ... as far as I know to date anyway ... but as you can see, I’ve already thought through what’d make this impossible thing possible and there’s only one missing link left <-!!> in the invisible chain there occupied by reality that I haven’t thought my way around yet — so who knows?? Fer now tho it’s this Google CSE thing. It ain’t great but we can improve it in sometimes substantial ways with stuff like this. There’s other XML-based config I haven’t touched yet. <-!!> The maximum number permitted is said to be 2000, tho it wasn’t clear to me that means 2000 elements or 2000 elements. This file’s inert on its own, btw; it needs to be sent to Google CSE if it changes and <-!!> we dunt have the kind of infrastructure here that’d permit us to spot this and automate its credentialed upload as a deployment step — and if we ever did it’d more or less follow that we *wouldn’t* still be using Google CSE.
attr
stat metric
BDY VIT vitality
INT intellect intelligence mental MND
CHA charisma CHR faith FTH SPI spiritual WIS wisdom
AP anima points animus
XP
HP
health points hit points
MP
magic points mana points skill points spell points SP
EXP experience experience points
ACC accuracy hit % hit percent hit rate
ATK POW power STR strength
CON constitution DEF defense END endurance STA stamina
M.ACC MACC magic accuracy
M.ATK MATK magic attack MPOW
magic defense M.DEF MDEF MDF WIL will willpower
FOR FORT fortitude FTD RES resilience
LCK Luck
AGI agility DEX dexterity dodge EV EVA evade reflexes
INIT
